behavior_summary: Provides NPC "Guard4" gameplay behavior: a short U8P_GUARD4::look/description, an area-trigger handler to reset or start AI, a periodic scheduler that can revive/reset the NPC, and a full conversation handler with a removable-question menu and one-time flags.
U8P_GUARD4::look: when the player examines the NPC it returns a short description. It checks Npc::isDead(this) and barks either 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman'.

U8P_GUARD4::enterFastArea: runs when an actor enters the guard's trigger region. If the NPC is dead it clears dead/in-combat flags and teleports the NPC to map 0x29 at (0x64,0x64) (inferred respawn/staging). If alive it calls engine AI/scheduling methods (METHOD::func091C then METHOD::func15E7) with suspend points to start/resume AI/behavior.

U8P_GUARD4::schedule: periodic housekeeping — initialize scheduling parameters, call METHOD::func143A and suspend; if process_result() is true then clear the dead flag and call METHOD::func133F (inferred revive/finalize).

U8P_GUARD4::use: full conversation flow. Initializes a menu array (local2), may immediately issue urgent 'Titans' warning lines if global danger flags hydrosFree/hasHeart/pyrosFree are set, then enters a remove-on-ask question loop that presents options, removes asked options, branches responses based on guard state (guard4Met, guard4Pissed, devonInRule) and sets per-guard flags (guard4Met, guard4Pissed, guard4Thanked) as appropriate. Dialogue uses urandom to occasionally vary a greeting and uses FREE::* spawn/reset calls for transient resources during conversation setup/teardown.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD4::look branches on Npc::isDead(this) to select a different short description text.
U8P_GUARD4::enterFastArea checks Npc::isDead(this): dead -> Npc::clrDead, Npc::clrInCombat, Npc::teleport(...); alive -> METHOD::func091C (suspend) then METHOD::func15E7 (suspend).

U8P_GUARD4::schedule sets several local scheduling parameters, calls METHOD::func143A and suspends; after resume it tests (word)process_result() and if true clears the dead flag and calls METHOD::func133F before suspending again.

U8P_GUARD4::use performs a conversation loop implemented with Ask/SuspendAssign and MenuRemove: local2 holds options, local1 captures the player's choice, options are removed as they are asked, and the loop exits when the player chooses 'Bye'.

One-time and stateful branches in dialogue depend on global flags guard4Met, guard4Pissed and guard4Thanked; these flags are read to vary greetings and written to record first-meet, annoyance, and one-time thanks.
open_questions: Exact runtime semantics of engine methods METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F — they appear related to AI start/U8P_GUARD4::schedule/finalize but precise effects are unknown.
Why U8P_GUARD4::enterFastArea teleports to map 0x29 at (0x64,0x64) — this is inferred as a respawn/staging position but the exact intended location meaning is not documented here.
Precise purpose of FREE::func1C19 spawned at conversation start and the parameters used with FREE::resetRef — likely resource/spawn bookkeeping but uncertain.
Thresholds and exact probability used with urandom(0x0A) > 0x05 — the presence of randomness is clear but exact variation in greeting frequency is approximate.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD4::look
main_function.purpose: Return a short description when the player examines this NPC; selects a 'dead' or 'alive' short description based on the NPC's dead state.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_GUARD4::look' or 'examine' action on this NPC.
Checks Npc::isDead(this) to pick which bark to emit.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::look
function_map.role: Provides the NPC's short description for player examine/U8P_GUARD4::look actions; branches on dead state.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger-area handler that either respawns/repositions a dead NPC (clear dead/in-combat + teleport) or kicks off normal AI via engine methods when alive.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler/housekeeping process that calls engine scheduling, suspends, and may revive/clean up the NPC when resumed based on process_result().

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::use
function_map.role: Conversation/dialogue handler presenting a removable-question menu, applying one-time/annoyance flags, and emitting urgent warning lines when global danger flags are set.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query NPC dead state (external engine method).

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Teleport NPC to a specific map/position.

function_map.name: METHOD::func091C
function_map.role: Engine/AI call invoked when starting/resuming behavior (exact semantics unknown).

function_map.name: METHOD::func15E7
function_map.role: Engine/AI call paired with func091C (exact semantics unknown).

function_map.name: METHOD::func143A
function_map.role: Engine scheduling/timer setup called by U8P_GUARD4::schedule before suspending.

function_map.name: METHOD::func133F
function_map.role: Engine call invoked after a positive process_result() in U8P_GUARD4::schedule; likely finalizes revive or reinitialization.

function_map.name: FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: Low-level spawn/free pool call observed during dialogue setup (purpose inferred as resource/spawn query).

function_map.name: FREE::resetRef
function_map.role: Free/resource reset call used after dialogue spawn operations.

function_map.name: process_result
function_map.role: Returns boolean result from a prior suspend/resume; used to decide post-suspend actions in U8P_GUARD4::schedule.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used to vary greeting branches in conversation.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to select an appropriate short description.
Decide whether to perform respawn/reset (if dead) or to start/resume AI (if alive).
Clear dead marker when reviving/respawning the NPC.
Clear in-combat flag as part of respawn/reset logic.
Move the NPC to an inferred respawn/staging location (map 0x29, x=0x64, y=0x64).
Start/update AI/behavior when NPC is alive (suspend/resume surrounding call); exact effect inferred.
Continuation/paired engine call to func091C during AI start/resume; exact effect inferred.
Invoke engine scheduling/timer setup before suspending the U8P_GUARD4::schedule process.
Check return from suspend to decide whether to clear dead flag and proceed with post-U8P_GUARD4::schedule actions.
Clear dead marker if the scheduler indicates (via process_result) that the NPC should be revived/reinitialized.
Spawn/free-pool call used during conversation setup (resource or temporary spawn query), observed via a spawn wrapper.
Reset/cleanup the temporary resource reference created during conversation setup.
Produce a random value to occasionally select an alternate greeting branch.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to an urgent-warning counter in U8P_GUARD4::use; presence increments a local counter that can trigger a 'Titans' warning line.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the urgent-warning counter and can cause an alternate immediate warning line in dialogue.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the urgent-warning counter and may trigger the 'Titans' warning line in conversation.

reads.name: guard4Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read in dialogue to choose gruff or annoyed reply variants; reflects whether the guard is currently annoyed at the player.

reads.name: guard4Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine greeting branch (different text if the player has already met the guard).

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Read to decide location-specific answers (Tempest vs. other area replies) and to influence a special thank-you branch on exit.

reads.name: guard4Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Read to avoid repeating a one-time gratitude/thank-you dialogue line.

writes.name: guard4Met
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player first speaks to this guard to record that the guard has met the player (affects future greeting choices).

writes.name: guard4Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Toggled in certain dialogue branches to mark or clear the guard's annoyed state; used to change subsequent replies.

writes.name: guard4Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard delivers a one-time 'thanks' reply so it is not repeated in later conversations.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides NPC "Guard4" gameplay behavior: a short U8P_GUARD4::look/description, an area-trigger handler to reset or start AI, a periodic scheduler that can revive/reset the NPC, and a full conversation handler with a removable-question menu and one-time flags.
